northStar: Players who complete a short fridge-organization session, replay for a cleaner result, and keep progressing through the campaign.

phases.gate: CrazyGames-ready browser build with stable English-first UX, clean first-session flow, and no blocker-level placement bugs in the first 10 levels.

tasks.title: Keep the review build English-first and portal-safe
acceptance: Review build defaults to English
Locale switching is hidden unless explicitly enabled
No broken locale route or white-screen behavior on static portals

tasks.title: Make level 1 a satisfying showcase level
acceptance: First level looks fuller than a prototype
A player can complete the first correct placement within a few seconds
The first level teaches a single clear rule without friction

tasks.title: Polish drag, snap, miss, and placement readability
acceptance: Correct placements feel magnetic and readable
Invalid drops communicate clearly without confusion
Door racks, shelves, and drawer placements feel visually seated

tasks.title: Add a real coin sink through an optional hint system
acceptance: Players can spend coins during a run
Hints reveal a valid next placement without auto-solving the level
Coins persist correctly after spending and winning

tasks.title: Remove leftover Pocket Planet naming and planning artifacts
acceptance: package metadata uses the Seedbound name
Sprint and autopilot files describe the fridge game instead of the old project
Status tooling reports relevant build freshness checks

phases.gate: Players consistently finish early levels, replay levels for cleaner outcomes, and use at least one light-progression loop such as hints or theme unlocks.

tasks.title: Expand the campaign to 50 polished levels
acceptance: The first 10 levels teach cleanly
Mid-game introduces spacing and ordering pressure
The full campaign avoids filler and repeated layouts

tasks.title: Add light cosmetic progression
acceptance: Players can unlock a visual reward with coins
Cosmetic unlocks do not interrupt the quick-session loop
Unlocked themes persist across sessions
